hook.text: Every product idea rests on assumptions. The dangerous ones are the assumptions you don't know you're making.

buildup.text: Assumption mapping lists every belief behind an idea: 'users have this problem,' 'they'll pay for a solution.'

discovery.text: Plot each assumption on two axes: how critical is it, and how much evidence do we have? The riskiest ones need testing.

twist.text: Teams often test the easiest assumptions instead of the riskiest. Comfort over impact — a discovery anti-pattern.

climax.text: Testing your riskiest assumption first is the most efficient way to reduce uncertainty in product development.

punchline.text: Find your riskiest assumption. Test that one first.

quiz.question: What should you test first in assumption mapping?
options: The riskiest assumption — high criticality with the least evidence
